mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M57 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M57 typically costs £1,500–£2,500 supply only [BMW-ESTIMATED], varying by variant and mileage. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M57 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M57 scores 4★ out of 5 with 90% confidence [BMW-VERIFIED]. It's widely regarded as one of BMW's most durable diesel engines, though swirl flap failure and injector wear are well-documented issues to address preventatively.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common BMW M57 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Swirl flap failure — the flaps can break off and enter the engine, causing catastrophic damage. Prevention is simple: fit blanking plates or a flap-free manifold. Typically occurs 80,000–120,000 miles [PATTERN DATA].

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the M57?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M57 powered the 3 Series (E46/E90 330d, 335d), 5 Series (E39/E60/F10 530d), 7 Series (E38/E65 730d), X3 (E83 3.0d) and X5 (E53/E70 3.0d/35d) across 1998–2013.

mainEntity.name: How long does the BMW M57 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes, swirl flap blanking, and injector care — the M57 regularly exceeds 200,000 miles. Many examples reach 250,000–300,000 miles without major rebuilds [THIRD-PARTY].

hero.h1: BMW M57 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M57 is a 3.0-litre straight-six turbodiesel — one of BMW's most durable engines, still sought after today. Backed by 830+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

rows.value: Also fitted to Land Rover (L322 Td6)

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on 830+ UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports [BMW-VERIFIED].

rows.score: 12 (Midpoint of Reconditioned supply range (£2,500–£4,500 → midpoint £3,500) — £3,000–£4,999 bracket → 16. (Wait — let me recalculate.) Reconditioned supply: £1,500–£2,500 (from EngineFinders data) → midpoint £2,000 → under £3,000 → 20)
16 (Most failures are well-understood, repairable issues — swirl flap blanking, injector replacement, turbo servicing)
20 (Parts are cheap and available from multiple UK suppliers same-day; M57 is one of the most common BMW diesel engines)
4 (Reconditioned cost (£2,000) as % of lowest typical host value (~£2,500 E39 530d) = 80% — exceeds brand scrap threshold)
76/100 (Wait — I need to verify the Reconditioned cost. Let me check: Search result 3 shows "Reconditioned M57: £1,500-£2,500". So midpoint is £2,000. That's under £3,000, so Repair Cost = 20.)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The M57 is one of BMW's most durable diesel sixes — swirl flap failure is the main preventable risk — buy with documented maintenance and budget for swirl flap blanking." (Clause 1: 8 words. Clause 2: 8 words. Clause 3: 8 words. Total: 24 words.)
verdictRating.bestFor: High-mileage motorway drivers who value durability and torque.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You regularly drive in London ULEZ — most M57s are Euro 3/4 non-compliant.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover Range Rover L322 Td6 → [landroverengine.uk/engines/m57/].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a BMW M57 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across BMW and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.
